files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 55c5b29..f95d053 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -2,25 +2,26 @@
 
 ## Strategy
-- **Segment Targeting:** S1 on Plan A ($34), S2 on Plan C ($174), S3 on Plan B ($134).
-- **Product Tiering:** Downgraded Plan A to Tier 1 model to force quality-conscious S2/S3 users to upgrade. Upgraded Plan B to Tier 3 to attract them.
-- **Spending:** Increased global Ops/Dev to $300/day each to clear support backlog and maintain quality. Targeted ad spend ($180/day) focusing on high-value S2/S3.
-- **Quotas:** Maintaining high quotas (A=2k, B=50k, C=250k) to prevent "quota wall" complaints, though social media suggests some users still feel constrained (possibly monthly limits or spikes).
+- **Segment Targeting:** S1 on Plan A ($34), S2 on Plan B ($129), S3 on Plan B/C.
+- **Product Tiering:** Moved Plan A to Tier 2 to fix conversion (was 1.4%). Plan B to Tier 4, Plan C to Tier 5 to attract S2/S3 and meet S2's high quality floor (0.37).
+- **Spending:** Increased global Ops/Dev to $500/day each to clear support backlog and maintain quality. Added $500/day targeted Dev for S2 to build permanent quality bonus.
+- **Enterprise:** Started LinkedIn ads ($150/day) for E1, E2, E3 to get enterprise leads.
+- **Research:** Conducted one market research ($25k) on Day 70.
 
-## Current Status (Day 63)
-- Cash: $62,064
-- Subs: 132 (67 S1, 27 S2, 38 S3).
-- **CRITICAL:** Most S2/S3 users are currently on Plan A ($29), which is sub-optimal. The tier change (Plan A -> Tier 1 model) is designed to move them to B/C.
-- Weekly Net Flow: ~-$5,500 (targeting growth to offset burn).
-- R&D: T1 and T2 COMPLETED.
+## Current Status (Day 70)
+- Cash: $55,901 (pre-research and pre-week 11 spend).
+- Subs: 130. 120 on Plan A.
+- **CRITICAL:** Conversion rate was very low (1/72 leads). Tier 1 Plan A was likely the cause.
+- S2 users (26) are on Plan A but are unhappy (quality < floor).
+- Support backlog is 8 issues; social media shows frustration.
 
-## Decisions for Week 10
-- Raised prices: A=$34, B=$134, C=$174.
-- Model tiers: A=1, B=3, C=4.
-- Ad spend: $30/day per (channel, group) for S2/S3 across Social, Search, and LinkedIn.
-- Cleared legacy targeted dev/ops spend.
+## Decisions for Week 11
+- Prices: A=$34, B=$129, C=$174.
+- Tiers: A=2, B=4, C=5.
+- Target Spend: S2 Dev $500/day, LinkedIn Ads $150/day for Enterprise.
+- Total Daily Burn: ~$2.2k (Sustainable for 2 weeks).
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-- Global dashboard only shows global spend; targeted spend must be tracked manually or via ledger.
-- S2/S3 will stay on Plan A if it meets their quality floor, even if Plan C is better. Forced differentiation via model tiers is necessary.
-- Support responsiveness is a key complaint; Ops spend must be sufficient to keep issues low.
-- Revenue lag: Monthly billing means price changes take 30 days to fully reflect in cash flow.
+- Tier 1 model is too low quality for most new leads, even price-sensitive ones.
+- S2 quality floor (0.37) is a hard constraint; they won't subscribe if it's not met.
+- Support responsiveness is a recurring issue in social media; Ops spend needs to stay high until backlog is zero.
+- Research is expensive ($25k) and risky; use sparingly when cash is low.
